text: Delivered 14 gale/Breeze JMH benchmark pairs (28 @Benchmark methods) in benchmarksJVM (Breeze 2.1.0 compile-scoped there only; core Breeze-free): BLAS-1 dot/axpy/norm (n up to 1M), BLAS-2 gemv/gemvT, BLAS-3 square/tall gemm + AtA, factorizations solve/LU/Cholesky/QR(no-Q vs justR), least squares vs dgels backslash, symmetric eigen eigSymmetric(All) vs eigSym. Identical seeded inputs both sides; JMH idioms matched (Throughput, Fork(2), Setup-built state, Blackhole). README documents sweep + -prof gc commands, pure-JVM netlib-fallback baseline (native BLAS = v0.5 target), smoke-compile-only session convention. benchCompile green (74 generated JMH sources); testAll 217+217+17+17 and parityTest 25 undisturbed. HEADLINE (unforked cold smokes, NOT rigorous): pure-Java F2J Breeze BEAT gale on dot (~2.9x) and gemm n=64 (~2.4x) — the outperform-pure-JVM-Breeze goal is NOT yet demonstrated; needs a real forked sweep (user-run) and likely kernel tuning (gemm block=64/64^3 threshold heuristics were already flagged untuned in the remediation handoff). Committed 39ac330.
